corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 401 unauthorized
corpus.body: An HTTP 401 unauthorized response means invalid or expired bearer token. To resolve a 401 error, check the request and retry. The 401 status is returned when invalid or expired bearer token.

corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 403 forbidden
corpus.body: An HTTP 403 forbidden response means insufficient role permissions. To resolve a 403 error, check the request and retry. The 403 status is returned when insufficient role permissions.

corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 404 not found
corpus.body: An HTTP 404 not found response means the resource identifier does not exist. To resolve a 404 error, check the request and retry. The 404 status is returned when the resource identifier does not exist.

corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 409 conflict
corpus.body: An HTTP 409 conflict response means a duplicate idempotency key was reused. To resolve a 409 error, check the request and retry. The 409 status is returned when a duplicate idempotency key was reused.

corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 422 validation failed
corpus.body: An HTTP 422 validation failed response means a required field was missing or malformed. To resolve a 422 error, check the request and retry. The 422 status is returned when a required field was missing or malformed.

corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 429 too many requests
corpus.body: An HTTP 429 too many requests response means the rate limit was exceeded. To resolve a 429 error, check the request and retry. The 429 status is returned when the rate limit was exceeded.

corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 500 server error
corpus.body: An HTTP 500 server error response means an unexpected internal error occurred. To resolve a 500 error, check the request and retry. The 500 status is returned when an unexpected internal error occurred.

corpus.title: Troubleshooting HTTP 503 service unavailable
corpus.body: An HTTP 503 service unavailable response means the service is temporarily down for maintenance. To resolve a 503 error, check the request and retry. The 503 status is returned when the service is temporarily down for maintenance.

corpus.title: Refund Policy
corpus.body: Refunds are issued within 14 days of purchase for unused subscriptions. Partial refunds apply to annual plans cancelled mid-term. Contact billing support to request a refund.

corpus.title: Privacy Policy
corpus.body: We collect account data, usage telemetry, and billing information. Personal data is retained for 24 months after account closure, then deleted.

corpus.title: Service Level Agreement
corpus.body: We guarantee 99.9 percent monthly uptime. Service credits apply when uptime falls below the threshold. Scheduled maintenance is excluded from the calculation.

corpus.title: Acceptable Use Policy
corpus.body: Accounts may not be used for spam, abuse, or illegal content. Violations result in suspension after a warning.
